result.stdout:
Usage:
  nulink [options] [command]

Options:
  --version    Display version information

Commands:
  status    Check status of packages in consumer project/solution
  link      Link package to a directory in local file system, machine-wide
  unlink    Unlink package from local file system, machine-wide

commands.payload:
Usage:
  nulink [options] [command]

Options:
  --version    Display version information

Commands:
  status    Check status of packages in consumer project/solution
  link      Link package to a directory in local file system, machine-wide
  unlink    Unlink package from local file system, machine-wide

result.stdout:
link:
  Link package to a directory in local file system, machine-wide

Usage:
  nulink link [options]

Options:
  -c, --consumer <file-path>    Path to consumer .csproj or .sln (default: first .csproj or .sln in current directory)
  -p, --package <package-id>    Package id (default: all packages in consumer project/solution)
  -l, --local <project-path>    Full path to package .csproj in local file system
  -d, --dry-run <on/off>        If specified, list intended actions without executing them

commands.payload:

Options:

  -l, --local <project-path>    Full path to package .csproj in local file system
  -d, --dry-run <on/off>        If specified, list intended actions without executing them

result.stdout:
status:
  Check status of packages in consumer project/solution

Usage:
  nulink status [options]

Options:
  -c, --consumer <file-path>    Path to consumer .csproj or .sln (default: first .csproj or .sln in current directory)
  -p, --package <package-id>    Package id (default: all packages in consumer project/solution)
  -q, --quiet <on/off>          If specified, suppresses all output except data (useful for scripting)
